interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, written communication, calm engineering
codingTopics: Table filtering and multi-column sort (sort, array, search)
systemDesignTopics: Design Coda's formula engine that recomputes dependent cells when a value changes
Design real-time collaborative editing for a Coda doc mixing text, tables, and formulas
Design a system that syncs external SaaS data into Coda tables via Packs
Design permissions and sharing for docs with fine-grained row and section access

coding: Coda docs have formulas that reference other cells. Given cell dependencies, recompute in the correct order and detect a circular reference.
Given a Coda table with filters and multi-column sorts, return the visible rows efficiently as the user edits filters.
Implement incremental recomputation: when one cell changes, recompute only the affected downstream cells.
Simulate a spreadsheet range formula (e.g., SUM over a filtered range) that updates as rows are added.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you built for a broad, flexible product surface.
Describe balancing power-user flexibility with simplicity for new users.
Tell me about a time you owned an ambiguous project end-to-end.
Describe a disagreement with a designer or PM and how you resolved it.
systemDesign: Design Coda's dependency-tracking formula engine so a single edit recomputes only what's affected across a large doc.
Design real-time multiplayer editing for a doc that combines rich text, tables, and live formulas.
Design 'Packs' that pull external SaaS data into tables and keep it fresh without hammering third-party APIs.
values: Coda blurs the line between docs and apps. When have you built a tool that changed how a team works?
How do you balance flexibility with an opinionated, simple user experience?
Tell me about deeply understanding a user's workflow before building.
other: How does Coda compare to Notion or Airtable?

Coda Interview Guide (2026): Document Collaboration Platform

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Coda
coda.io ↗

Coda is the document collaboration platform that combines docs, spreadsheets, and apps — competing with Notion, Airtable, and Google Workspace. The interview emphasizes complex data modeling, real-time collaboration, and the unique formula engine that powers Coda's flexibility.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding pair → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a flexible doc-table-app data model

- Design a formula engine with cross-document references

- Design real-time collaboration with rich data structures

- Coding: medium DSA, often with graph or expression-tree framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, written communication, calm engineering

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $170K–$210K total

- Senior SE: $250K–$330K

- Staff: $360K–$480K

- Principal: $490K–$650K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in TypeScript (frontend + backend)

- Understand collaborative document patterns (CRDT, OT)

- Brush up on expression evaluation and formula engines

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Coda remote-friendly?

Distributed-first. Hubs in San Francisco and Bellevue. Most engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Coda compare to Notion or Airtable?

Notion has the largest brand. Coda has more powerful formula and app-building. Airtable specializes in data tables. Coda pays competitively with mid-tier SaaS.

What is the engineering culture?

Calm, technically deep, written-communication-heavy. Strong fit for engineers who like complex systems and writing.
